# UC-21.4.9 · Last-Mile Delivery SLA Compliance

## Description

Last-mile delivery performance drives customer experience and contract compliance. Tracking on-time rates enables route optimization and exception management.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Last-Mile Delivery SLA Compliance to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Integrate delivery completion events with promised timestamps. Calculate on-time delivery percentage daily. Alert on drops below SLA threshold. Analyze late deliveries by route and driver. **Domain context:** Last-mile SLAs often exclude force majeure; “promised_time” may be customer slot vs carrier internal SLA—document definitions. **Splunk:** `actual_time` and `promised_time` must be epoch-consistent; `status="delivered"` should exclude cancelled/returned.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Delivery management platform via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="delivery:event"` fields `delivery_id`, `promised_time`, `actual_time`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate delivery completion events with promised timestamps. Calculate on-time delivery percentage daily. Alert on drops below SLA threshold. Analyze late deliveries by route and driver. **Domain context:** Last-mile SLAs often exclude force majeure; “promised_time” may be customer slot vs carrier internal SLA—document definitions. **Splunk:** `actual_time` and `promised_time` must be epoch-consistent; `status="delivered"` should exclude cancelled/returned.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="delivery:event" status="delivered"
| eval on_time=if(actual_time <= promised_time, 1, 0)
| eval late_min=if(on_time=0, round((actual_time-promised_time)/60,1), 0)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats avg(on_time) as otd_rate, avg(late_min) as avg_late_min, count as deliveries by _time
| eval otd_pct=round(otd_rate*100,2)
| table _time, deliveries, otd_pct, avg_late_min
```

Understanding this SPL

**Last-Mile Delivery SLA Compliance** — Last-mile delivery performance drives customer experience and contract compliance. Tracking on-time rates enables route optimization and exception management.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="delivery:event"` fields `delivery_id`, `promised_time`, `actual_time`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Delivery management platform via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: logistics; **sourcetype**: delivery:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=logistics, sourcetype="delivery:event". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **on_time** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **late_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **otd_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Last-Mile Delivery SLA Compliance**): table _time, deliveries, otd_pct, avg_late_min


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the fleet telematics or TMS portal and, for warehouse or port flows, the WMS or terminal operations screen for the same vehicle, container, or order window. Align GPS time zones and odometer units with the source system.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (OTD trend), Gauge (current OTD %), Bar chart (late deliveries by reason).

## SPL

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="delivery:event" status="delivered"
| eval on_time=if(actual_time <= promised_time, 1, 0)
| eval late_min=if(on_time=0, round((actual_time-promised_time)/60,1), 0)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats avg(on_time) as otd_rate, avg(late_min) as avg_late_min, count as deliveries by _time
| eval otd_pct=round(otd_rate*100,2)
| table _time, deliveries, otd_pct, avg_late_min
```

## Visualization

Line chart (OTD trend), Gauge (current OTD %), Bar chart (late deliveries by reason).
